product_overview: Lume is a game-forward social app for meeting strangers via ephemeral real-time text chat and lightweight two-player games. Users sign up with email, complete a brief onboarding, enter a matchmaking queue, chat, invite to games inline, and can report or block for safety.
core_goals: Meet strangers safely with an 18+ gate, required Terms & Privacy consent, and first-class reporting/blocking.
Match users based on shared interests (with region and age proximity as tiebreakers) while ensuring fallback matches so wait times are short.
Keep conversations ephemeral — messages are broadcast in real time and are not persisted to the database.
Make the first minute engaging by providing inline two-player games that run inside the chat.
Provide a simple, responsive experience across mobile and desktop with a persistent light/dark theme.

features.name: Email Signup
features.description: Create a new account with email and password; successful signup auto-signs the user in and routes them to onboarding.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Switch to the Sign up tab on /login -> Fill email -> Fill password (meets complexity rules) -> Submit signup form -> Be redirected to /onboarding

Navigate to /login -> Switch to the Sign up tab on /login -> Fill an already-registered email -> Fill password -> Submit signup form -> See inline validation error about existing account and remain on /login

features.name: Email Login
features.description: Sign in with email and password; routes the user to the dashboard if onboarding is complete or to onboarding if not.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Fill email -> Fill password -> Submit login form -> Be redirected to /dashboard (if onboarding complete)
Navigate to /login -> Fill email of an account with incomplete profile -> Fill password -> Submit login form -> Be redirected to /onboarding
Navigate to /login -> Fill email -> Fill incorrect password -> Submit login form -> See inline error about invalid credentials and remain on /login

features.name: Password Reset
features.description: Request a password reset and set a new password via the recovery link captured locally.
user_flows: Navigate to /forgot-password -> Enter account email -> Submit password reset request -> See success confirmation
Open the recovery link captured locally (Mailpit) -> Navigate to /reset-password -> Enter and confirm new password -> Submit new password -> Be signed in and redirected to /dashboard

features.name: Logout
features.description: Sign the current user out and return them to the public landing page; protected pages require login afterward.
user_flows: Be signed in and on /dashboard -> Initiate sign out from the dashboard -> Be redirected to / -> Navigate to /dashboard -> See redirect to /login

features.name: Onboarding
features.description: Post-signup profile setup (display name, DOB enforcing 18+, gender, optional region, interest tags, and Terms & Privacy consent).
user_flows: Be redirected to /onboarding after signup -> Enter a display name of 2–30 characters -> Pick a DOB that proves 18+ -> Select a gender -> Optionally enter a region -> Toggle 1–8 interest tags -> Check Terms & Privacy consent -> Submit onboarding form -> Be redirected to /dashboard
Navigate to /onboarding -> Enter a DOB under 18 -> Attempt to submit onboarding form -> See inline error preventing submission
Navigate to /onboarding -> Select 0 interest tags -> Attempt to submit onboarding form -> See inline error requiring at least 1 interest
Navigate to /onboarding -> Leave Terms & Privacy unchecked -> Attempt to submit onboarding form -> See inline error requiring consent

features.name: Lobby and Matchmaking Queue
features.description: Primary authenticated lobby where users choose Chat or Games, tune interest chips, and enter the matchmaking queue to be paired with another user.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> See time-aware greeting and a live online counter -> Toggle match mode between Chat and Games -> Optionally toggle interest chips -> Start matching -> See searching view with a cancel option -> Cancel searching -> Return to idle lobby view
Navigate to /dashboard -> Configure mode and interests -> Start matching -> When a match is found be routed to /chat

features.name: Realtime Chat
features.description: One-on-one ephemeral chat with presence, typing indicator, elapsed timer, and explicit end/disconnect states.
user_flows: Navigate to /chat -> See the stranger's display name and the elapsed timer in the header -> Type in the input and observe typing indicator activity for the peer -> Send a message and see it appear immediately for both sides -> End chat from either side -> See the chat-ended view with duration and rematch/report options -> Return to /dashboard
Navigate to /chat -> Peer disconnects unexpectedly -> Observe disconnected state and auto-return to /dashboard

features.name: Inline Game Panel in Chat
features.description: Invite the chat peer to a two-player game and play inline beside the conversation without leaving the chat route.
user_flows: Navigate to /chat -> Open the Game Panel -> Pick a game from the picker -> Send a game invite -> Peer accepts the invite -> Play the game side-by-side while chatting -> Finish the round -> Choose rematch or exit back to chat
Navigate to /chat -> Open the Game Panel -> Pick a game from the picker -> Send a game invite -> Peer declines the invite -> Dismiss invite and remain in chat

features.name: Games Catalogue
features.description: Browse available and coming-soon games from a dedicated catalogue and select a game to preselect it for matchmaking.
user_flows: Navigate to /games -> See sections for Available and Coming soon games -> Hover or focus a game card to view description, players, and duration -> Select an available game -> Be returned to /dashboard with that game preselected for matchmaking

features.name: Tic Tac Toe (example game)
features.description: 3×3 two-player Tic Tac Toe played inline with instant sync, win-line highlighting, draw detection, and rematch.
user_flows: In /chat with an active game session -> Accept a Tic Tac Toe invite -> On your turn place a mark in an empty cell -> Observe opponent's mark appear after their turn -> When three-in-a-row occurs see the win-line highlighted and the round end state -> Choose rematch or exit to chat

features.name: Report User
features.description: Flag the current chat peer with a required reason, optional notes, and a default-on Also block checkbox; submitting creates a report and blocks the peer.
user_flows: While in /chat or on the chat-ended view -> Open the report dialog -> Pick one of the reasons -> Optionally add notes up to 1000 chars -> Leave Also block checked -> Submit the report -> See confirmation and return to /dashboard
While in /chat -> Open the report dialog -> Attempt to submit without choosing a reason -> See submit disabled or inline validation preventing submission

features.name: Block and Unblock
features.description: Silently block a user so they will not be matched again; blocked users can be unblocked later from settings.
user_flows: While in /chat -> Block the peer via the report flow or chat actions -> Observe chat end and return to /dashboard -> Re-attempt matchmaking and confirm the blocked user is filtered out
Navigate to /settings -> Open blocking management (unblock list) -> Unblock a previously blocked user -> Confirm the unblock action removes the user from the local exclusion list

features.name: Settings
features.description: Edit profile fields (display name, DOB, gender, region) and matching interests; saves persist to the profile and reflect in the lobby.
user_flows: Navigate to /settings -> See current profile prefilled in the form -> Edit display name, DOB, gender, or region -> Save profile changes -> See success toast and refreshed profile data
Navigate to /settings -> Toggle interest tags in preferences -> Save preferences -> See success toast and have lobby reflect updated interests
Navigate to /settings -> Enter a DOB under 18 -> Attempt to save -> See inline validation error preventing save

features.name: Theme Toggle
features.description: Switch between light and dark themes; selection persists to localStorage and avoids a flash on reload.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Toggle the theme control in the header -> Observe immediate palette swap -> Reload the page -> Confirm the previously selected theme persists and there is no light-then-dark flash

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public marketing landing site that introduces the product, explains how it works, and links to auth pages and legal content.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See hero, features, comparison, how-it-works, FAQ, and CTA -> Scroll through sections and observe smooth anchor scroll for nav links -> Use the Sign in CTA to navigate to /login

tech_stack: TanStack Query (server state with SSR dehydration)
Supabase (Auth, Realtime Broadcast, Presence, Edge Functions, pg_cron)

routes.description: Landing page (hero, features, comparison, how-it-works, FAQ, CTA)
Combined sign in and sign up with tabs
Signs the user out and redirects to the landing page
Set a new password from the email recovery link
18+ age gate, display name, DOB, gender, region, interest tags, Terms & Privacy consent
Lobby / matching view with hero match card, games rail, conversation starters, and vibe preview
1-on-1 realtime chat with inline game panel, safety controls, and elapsed timer
Games catalogue listing 7 available and 2 coming-soon games
Edit profile (display name, DOB, gender, region) and matching preferences (interests)

Fill password input
Click Sign in
See error toast on invalid credentials
On success, be routed to /dashboard (or /onboarding if incomplete)

user_interactions: Enter email on /forgot-password
Submit the form and see a success confirmation

features.description: Sign the current user out and redirect to the landing page

Click Logout
Confirm redirection to the landing page
Confirm protected routes now redirect to /login

Pick a DOB that proves 18+ via the calendar popover
Select a gender (male, female, non-binary, prefer-not-to-say)

user_interactions: See time-aware greeting and live online counter
Toggle match mode between chat and games
Optionally toggle one-tap interest chips
Click Start matching to enqueue
See the searching view with a cancel option
Get routed to /chat when a match is found

api_calls.endpoint: Edge Function match-users (invoked by pg_cron every 2s)

features.name: Realtime Chat

user_interactions: See the stranger's display name and elapsed timer in the header
Type in the input and see the typing indicator on the peer side
Send a message and see it appear instantly on both sides
Click End chat to terminate the session
See the chat-ended view with rematch and report options
Observe auto-return to /dashboard if the peer disconnects

api_calls.endpoint: Supabase Realtime Broadcast (chat-room channel messages and typing events)

features.name: Inline Game Panel in Chat
features.description: Invite the peer to a game from chat; plays side-by-side over Broadcast without leaving the chat route
user_interactions: Click the games icon in the chat header or picker card
Pick a game from the modal
Peer sees the invite and accepts or declines
Play the game side-by-side while chatting
Finish the round and choose rematch or exit game

api_calls.endpoint: Supabase Realtime Broadcast (game invite, moves, rematch on chat-room channel)

features.name: Games Catalogue
features.description: Browse the 7 available and 2 coming-soon games from a dedicated catalogue route
user_interactions: Visit /games from the sidebar or games rail
See two sections Available and Coming soon
Hover or focus a card to see description, players, and duration
Click an available game to go back to the lobby with that game preselected

features.name: Tic Tac Toe
features.description: 3x3 two-player game, synced over Broadcast, with win line highlighting and rematch
user_interactions: Accept a tic-tac-toe invite
Click a cell on your turn to place a mark
See opponent's mark appear after their turn
See win line highlight or draw banner when the round ends
Choose rematch or exit

features.name: Trivia
features.description: Six-question multiple choice trivia with blind answers and synced reveal
user_interactions: Accept a trivia invite
Pick one of four answers per question
Wait for the peer to answer, then see the simultaneous reveal
See scores after six rounds with rematch or exit option

features.name: Would You Rather
features.description: Eight either-or prompts with blind votes and synced reveal
user_interactions: Accept a would-you-rather invite
Pick option A or B on each prompt
See the peer's pick after both commit
Finish all prompts and see alignment summary

features.name: Rock Paper Scissors
features.description: Best-of-five with blind commit and simultaneous reveal
user_interactions: Accept an RPS invite
Click rock, paper, or scissors each round
See the reveal only after both have committed
Win best-of-five and choose rematch

features.name: Two Truths and a Lie
features.description: Take turns authoring three statements; the peer guesses which one is the lie
user_interactions: Accept a two-truths invite
Author two truths and one lie as the current author
Peer picks which statement is the lie
See the reveal and swap author roles for the next round

features.name: Emoji Charades
features.description: Author describes a prompt in emojis; peer types the guess
user_interactions: Accept an emoji charades invite
As author, pick a prompt and compose an emoji sequence
As guesser, see only the emojis and type a guess
See the reveal and swap roles

features.name: Draw and Guess
features.description: Drawer picks a difficulty and sketches on a live canvas; guesser types guesses until correct or time runs out
user_interactions: Accept a draw-and-guess invite
Drawer picks difficulty (easy, medium, hard)
Drawer sketches on the canvas; peer sees strokes in near-real-time
Guesser types guesses in the input
Round ends on correct guess or timeout, then swap roles

api_calls.endpoint: Supabase Broadcast (game moves and customEvents stroke sync)

features.name: Report User
features.description: Flag the current chat peer with a reason and optional notes; also block by default
user_interactions: Click the Flag icon in the chat header or end-of-chat screen
Pick one of seven reasons (harassment, nudity, spam, underage, hate_speech, self_harm, other)
Optionally add notes (up to 1000 chars)
Leave the Also block checkbox on by default
Submit and see a confirmation; chat ends immediately

features.name: Block and Unblock
features.description: Silently block a user so they never match again; unblock from settings if desired
user_interactions: Trigger block from the report dialog or chat actions menu
Confirm chat ends and you return to /dashboard
Try matchmaking again and confirm the blocked user is filtered out

features.name: Settings
features.description: Edit display name, DOB, gender, region, and matching interests
user_interactions: Open /settings
Edit display name, DOB, gender, or region
Save the profile section and see a success toast
Toggle interest tags in the preferences section
Save and see a success toast; lobby reflects the new interests

features.name: Theme Toggle
features.description: Light/dark theme toggle persisted to localStorage with no flash on reload
user_interactions: Click the sun/moon toggle in the header or dashboard topbar
Confirm immediate palette swap
Reload the page and confirm the theme persists
Confirm there is no light-then-dark flash on reload

features.name: Landing Page
features.description: Public marketing page with hero, features, comparison, how-it-works, FAQ, and CTA
user_interactions: Load the landing page and see the hero CTA
Scroll through features, comparison, how-it-works, FAQ sections
Click nav links and confirm smooth anchor scroll
Click Sign in to navigate to /login

known_limitations.issue: TestSprite drives a single browser, so any flow that requires two live users (matchmaking pairing, realtime chat, inline games) cannot be fully exercised end-to-end in a single run
known_limitations.impact: Tests can verify queue entry, UI states, and single-side rendering, but cannot confirm peer reception of Broadcast events without a second driven session

known_limitations.issue: Local Supabase is required to be running (npm run db:start and npm run db:reset) before the test suite runs; .env must point VITE_SUPABASE_URL at http://127.0.0.1:54321
known_limitations.impact: If Supabase is not running or the DB is not seeded, auth and matchmaking tests will fail with connection errors

known_limitations.issue: Password reset requires opening an email from Mailpit at http://127.0.0.1:54324, which TestSprite's browser cannot access cross-origin automatically
known_limitations.impact: The request-reset step can be validated, but the confirm-new-password step needs either a manual relay or a Supabase admin API bypass

known_limitations.issue: Matchmaking relies on a pg_cron job invoking the match-users edge function every 2 seconds; cron is enabled in the local migration but can be flaky on first boot
known_limitations.impact: Initial matchmaking may take 2-4 seconds or longer; tests should tolerate this latency

known_limitations.issue: The chat session has no server-enforced timeout, so test runs must explicitly end or disconnect sessions to avoid leaking matches
known_limitations.impact: Long-running tests can hold open sessions; always call end chat in teardown
